claims.text: Company X meets SOC2 Type II requirements across all evaluated areas
claims.verification_requirement: Independent SOC2 Type II audit by a licensed CPA firm covering all five Trust Service Criteria; review of audit scope to confirm 'all evaluated areas' encompasses complete SOC2 requirements
claims.risk_if_false: Company X proceeds to a formal SOC2 audit expecting to pass and fails, resulting in audit costs, remediation expenses, reputational damage, and potential loss of customer contracts

claims.text: Multi-factor authentication is enforced across ALL systems
claims.verification_requirement: Complete inventory of all systems in scope; technical verification (e.g., IAM logs, SSO configuration, policy enforcement records) confirming MFA cannot be bypassed on every system, including legacy systems, service accounts, and administrative interfaces
claims.risk_if_false: Unprotected systems create breach vectors; SOC2 auditors flag MFA gaps as findings, resulting in a qualified or adverse audit opinion

claims.text: AES-256 encryption is applied to all data at rest
claims.verification_requirement: Configuration documentation and technical evidence (e.g., storage encryption settings, database encryption configs, backup encryption policies) covering every data store, endpoint, and backup medium in scope
claims.risk_if_false: Unencrypted data stores expose sensitive customer data; SOC2 auditors identify the gap; potential regulatory liability and breach notification obligations

claims.text: TLS 1.3 is used for ALL data in transit
claims.verification_requirement: Network scanning results, TLS configuration records, and traffic analysis confirming no connections use TLS 1.2 or lower, including internal service-to-service traffic, APIs, and third-party integrations
claims.risk_if_false: Downgrade attacks possible on non-TLS-1.3 connections; auditors flag as a finding; data in transit may be intercepted

claims.text: A documented incident response plan exists
claims.verification_requirement: Review of the actual incident response plan document, including version control history, approval signatures, and distribution records confirming it is current and accessible to relevant staff
claims.risk_if_false: Absence or inadequacy of documentation results in SOC2 audit finding; in a real incident, uncoordinated response increases breach severity and regulatory exposure

claims.text: The incident response plan is tested quarterly
claims.verification_requirement: Test records, tabletop exercise logs, or drill reports covering at least four test cycles within the audit period, including dates, participants, scenarios used, and any remediation actions taken
claims.risk_if_false: Untested plans are ineffective; SOC2 auditors require evidence of testing and flag its absence; company is unprepared for actual incidents

claims.text: Company X is ready for a SOC2 audit
claims.verification_requirement: Gap assessment by a qualified SOC2 readiness firm or licensed CPA, covering all five Trust Service Criteria (Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, Privacy) with evidence sampled across the full audit period
claims.risk_if_false: Company invests in audit fees and fails; customers relying on this assessment to make trust or procurement decisions are misled; potential contractual and legal liability

limitations: This analysis cannot verify whether the automated assessment tool had access to actual technical configurations or relied on self-reported questionnaire responses
This analysis cannot confirm the scope of 'all evaluated areas' — whether it covers all five SOC2 Trust Service Criteria or only a subset
This analysis cannot assess the audit period covered (SOC2 Type II requires a minimum observation period, typically 6-12 months)
This analysis cannot evaluate whether the assessor is qualified or independent, which is required for a valid SOC2 opinion
This analysis cannot check for gaps in areas not mentioned (e.g., availability, processing integrity, privacy, vendor management, change management, logical access reviews)
This analysis cannot determine whether the assessment reflects point-in-time configuration checks versus sustained operational controls over time
This analysis cannot verify the accuracy of the generation date or whether the assessment reflects current system states
